pattern: Because the array wraps around, grouping the ones is really about finding the fixed-size window (equal to the ones-count) that already holds the most ones, wherever it sits -- including one that straddles the end and the start.
hints: The number of ones never changes, so think about the size of the block of positions you'd eventually want all of them to occupy.
Consider a window whose length equals the total count of ones, and slide it around the array -- including positions that wrap past the last index back to the first.
The fewest swaps equal the ones missing from your best possible window: the total ones minus the largest number of ones any same-length window already contains.

edge_inputs.description: a typical mix needing a couple of swaps to consolidate
smallest possible array, a single one already grouped
smallest possible array, no ones at all
ones already contiguous without needing the wraparound, so zero swaps are required
ones sit at the very start and very end -- circularly adjacent, so they are already grouped with zero swaps, which a non-circular solution would miss
strictly alternating pattern, the worst case for scattering, forcing the maximum useful swaps
every position is already a one

stress.description: random binary arrays across the full allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
